excludedSurfaces.app-partner: No partner-owned address or geofence mutation is part of JRN-006.
excludedSurfaces.app-captain: Captain navigation consumes later delivery-location journeys and does not own client address truth.
excludedSurfaces.app-field: Field onboarding and assignments do not own client delivery-address truth.
excludedSurfaces.webapp: No separate governed client-address runtime is registered.
excludedSurfaces.website: Public website does not process authenticated delivery addresses.

capabilities: Provider-backed search and reverse geocoding remain behind DSH routes.
Map results expose verified service-area readback.
Client address create and update require coordinates resolving to the supplied active geofence.
Database triggers reject direct or racing writes that bypass backend validation.
Create idempotency replay remains valid after a previous successful write.
Expired or deleted address PII follows governed retention and anonymization paths.
negativeInvariants: No client surface calls a map provider directly.
No client-supplied serviceAreaCode is accepted as serviceability truth without geofence resolution.
No active address write succeeds without latitude and longitude.
No active address write succeeds against an inactive or mismatched geofence.
No surface owns a second service-area or address business-logic implementation.
No runtime mock or fallback may establish map, address, or privacy truth.
No cross-actor address PII access is permitted.
acceptanceConditions: Static JRN-006 integrity test passes on the exact commit.
JRN-006 Go ownership packages compile and pass on the exact commit.
PostgreSQL 16 applies the relevant migrations and passes positive, negative, update, inactive-zone, and soft-delete cases.
Independent Product, QA/runtime, and Security/Privacy reviews remain required before CLOSED_WITH_EVIDENCE.
